parameters.name: vdd
parameters.override_behavior: sets the voltage-coded clk_out high level

parameters.name: vth
parameters.override_behavior: sets the threshold for jitter_en and all seed inputs

parameters.name: tr
parameters.override_behavior: sets clk_out rise and fall smoothing

modeling_constraints: AMS role: deterministic jittered clock stimulus source for repeatable timing-margin and aperture-stress checks.
Use deterministic timer-driven state and no random distribution functions.
Decode seed inputs only through vth and preserve repeatability for identical input histories.
Do not add undeclared ports, random state, or validation-only timing cases.

properties.observable_contract: With jitter_en below vth, clk_out has a constant 10 ns half-period and 20 ns full period.

Seed7:seed0 is sampled through vth only at each output transition and interpreted as one unsigned eight-bit seed with seed7 as MSB; seed changes affect the next scheduled half-period, not the already scheduled current edge.
For edge index k, the next half-period is 10 ns plus (((seed + 3*k) modulo 5) minus 2) times 0.8 ns.
With constant seed and enable history, the modulo-5 half-period sequence repeats every five output transitions; identical input histories also produce the same edge-time sequence on repeated runs.
Every jitter-enabled half-period remains within the public modulation range from 8.4 ns through 11.6 ns.
clk_out uses 0 V and vdd levels with finite transition smoothing set by tr.

title: Deterministic Jittered Clock Source
